canto_title: The Tale of Count Ugolino and the Frozen Souls

scenes.summary: Ugolino ceases his gruesome feeding and prepares to speak of his grief.
Ugolini identifies himself and his companion, Archbishop Ruggieri, to Dante.
Ugolino begins his narrative, describing how he was trapped and the onset of hunger.
Ugolino describes the tower and the Pisan lords watching over the prisoners.
The tragedy unfolds as Ugolino’s children wake up hungry and he remains stoically silent.
A sudden light reveals the children's horrific suggestion that Ugolino eat them to survive.
The narrative recounts the slow, agonizing starvation and deaths of Ugolino's sons.
The poem cuts back to the present, showing Ugolino returning to gnawing on the skull.
Ugolino directs a vitriolic curse against the city of Pisa and names other victims.
Dante and Virgil move into the frozen lake, where Dante observes the rain of tears.
A frozen soul in the ice begs Dante to remove the ice from his face.
The soul identifies himself as Fra Alberigo and explains the nature of his sin.
Alberigo explains how the souls of the treacherous fall into the pit while their bodies remain above.
Dante expresses doubt regarding the soul's claims, leading to a technical explanation of the Malebranche.
Dante concludes the canto with a sharp condemnation of the people of Genoa.
